headline: CrowdStrike Stock Isn’t Done Gaining, Analyst Says. AI Threats Offer Upside.
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings  stock was rising on Tuesday after a TD Cowen analyst cited confidence that the cybersecurity company’s capabilities will continue to be in high demand due to the risks that artificial intelligence presents.  Shaul Eyal hiked his price target for  CrowdStrike  stock to $625 from $480 while maintaining a Buy rating on Tuesday.  “As the category-leading vendor of endpoint protection to secure AI workflows, we see CRWD as very well positioned to benefit disproportionately from rising global cybersecurity budgets as automated threat detection and response becomes a foundational survival equipment,” Eyal wrote.

headline: DZ Bank Just Slapped CrowdStrike With a Sell Rating. Is the $700 Bull Case Falling Apart?

headline: US software stocks rebound, seeking to loosen AI's grip
summary: Shares of several U.S. software stocks gained on Tuesday, as the industry attempts a comeback after being battered for much of ‌the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF hit its highest level since January before paring gains, while Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce rose between 1.4% and 2.4%.  The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.

The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.

headline: Why Zscaler’s Recent Rally Could Just Be Getting Started
summary: Zscaler (NASDAQ: ZS) stock has been on a roller coaster. The stock hit an all-time high of $368 in 2021. Even after a strong rebound recently, it still trades around $175. That means investors who bought near the top are still deep underwater, even as the stock has surged roughly 30% in the past month alone.
